Annotation

Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us
The patient shows elevated serum creatinine, low total CO₂, low alkaline phosphatase, low serum globulin, and significant proteinuria, glycosuria, ketonuria, and bilirubinuria. Urine is cloudy, brown, with low specific gravity. These findings suggest impaired renal function (possible acute or chronic kidney disease with tubular dysfunction), metabolic acidosis, and possible liver involvement. Prompt nephrology and hepatology evaluation is recommended.
